creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
> … many of them are using FreeBSD.

<http://uptime.netcraft.com/perf/reports/Hosters> redirects to 
<https://uptime.netcraft.com/perf/reports/Hosters> where, more accurately, only one site uses FreeBSD. With all due respect, this is not good advocacy. 


The second link <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/#introduction-nutshell-users> no longer leads to a list of sites using FreeBSD. 


I reckon: 

a) remove the bullet point for Netcraft; and

b) add a point with reference to <https://freebsdfoundation.org/freebsd-project/what-is-freebsd/>, where we have (amongst other things): 

> Who is Using FreeBSD?

– I'll send an e-mail to the Foundation, for their page to more accurately refer to <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/introduction/#introduction-nutshell-users> (instead of outdated <https://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/handbook/nutshell.html#introduction-nutshell-users>). 

----

Better, IMHO: remove both bullet points, then have #_sites_using_freebsd anchor to a simple sentence that includes the link to the Foundation page. 

(Foundation staff are well-placed to co-ordinate advocacy activities. Give yourselves one less area to maintain at the FreeBSD web site.)
